comments: The vintage aesthetic here is well-executed but the underlying image structure doesn't hold up to forensic scrutiny. The film grain is the main giveaway — it measures at a spatial correlation of 0.08, meaning it's random per-pixel noise rather than real analog film grain which always clusters above 0.4. Real film grain has a physical texture from the silver halide crystal structure; digitally-simulated or AI-generated grain doesn't. The hue histogram entropy is 2.4, reflecting a compressed color palette consistent with AI color grading rather than natural mid-century color film. There are also three faces in the background crowd that share near-identical geometric structure — a diffusion model population artifact. The primary subject's clothing physics and shadow consistency are convincing, which is why confidence is moderate rather than high.
metadata: No authentic EXIF data. Vintage aesthetic is synthetically applied — underlying noise structure does not contain genuine analog film characteristics. No scanner ICC profile, no film stock color profile. Aspect ratio and file size consistent with Midjourney vintage photography output.
spectral: 17 spectral spikes (score 1.79). Grain spatial correlation 0.08 — synthetic. Hue entropy 2.4 — compressed. Background face repetition at 3 instances. PRNU 0.12. Radial PSD slope -1.41.
